html:
Axis to project
Demo steps
⚠ Figure failed to load

plan.contextChunk:
Simply placing them at evenly spaced intervals along the axis in the
image won't be correct. Due to geometric distortion introduced by
perspective projection, the tick marks are not evenly spaced within the
image plane. This distortion affects each axis in a distinct manner.

Let's start assuming we know the location of some arbitrary 3D distance
to the origin on each axis that we will denote by $\alpha_X$, $\alpha_Y$
and $\alpha_Z$. The question is, where do we place the ticks for
$k\alpha_X$, $k\alpha_Y$ and $k\alpha_Z$ for all $k$? We will use the
cross-ratio to calibrate the projection of the world coordinate system
in the image plane. For doing this we will need the location of the
horizon line of a reference plane (i.e. the ground plane), the position
of the orthogonal vanishing point and the real measure of one object in
the scene.

As shown in @fig-axis_calibration_procedure (a) we can use the cross-ratio
to estimate the location of the points $Y= k \alpha_X$ where $\alpha_X$
is an arbitrary constant as shown in @fig-axis_calibration_procedure (b),
and the corresponding image location $r$, for $k=1$, is chosen
arbitrarily as shown in @fig-axis_calibration_procedure (a). Due to
perspective projection, the image projection of the point
$Y= k \alpha_X$ will not be evenly spaced in the image. In this
particular example we chose the orientation of the world axis in image
coordinates to be aligned with the location of the vanishing points.

![Using the cross-ratio invariant to project the world coordinates into the image using only the vanishing points.](figures/single_view_3d/axis_calibration_procedure.png){width="100%" #fig-axis_calibration_procedure}

In order to calibrate the axis we need to know the dimensions of an
object in the scene. We need measurements along the three axis. In this
example, we used the bookshelf height (197 cm) and the radius of the
table base (50 cm). Using these reference measurements we can solve for
the image locations of the ticks $\alpha_X=\alpha_Y=\alpha_Z=1$ m.

@fig-office_all_calibrated_box shows the final calibrated axes with
ticks placed each 50 cm along each of the three axes.

![Calibrated world axes. Each axis is calibrated using the measurement of a scene element parallel to the world-coordinate axis. In this example, we used the bookshelf height (197 cm) and the radius of the table base (50 cm). From the calibrated axes, you can see that the office is around 250 cm wide.](figures/single_view_3d/office_all_calibrated_box.png){width="100%" #fig-office_all_calibrated_box}

From this calibrated system we can directly read the height of the
camera. The location of the camera coincides with the location of the
horizon line because, in this example, the ground plane is horizontal.

elements: 3D world coordinate axes X_W (blue), Y_W (red), Z_W (green) originating at O
Points P1, P2, P3, P4 marked along world axes
Image plane shown at an oblique angle
Projection lines (dashed) connecting 3D points P1–P4 to their respective projections on the image plane
Projected points v1 (blue), v2 (green), v3 (red) on the image plane
Horizon line of the ground plane drawn in the image plane
Orthogonal vanishing point marked in the image plane
3D world points spacing annotation: segment lengths labeled α_X, α_Y, α_Z
World axis tick marks spaced at k·α_X, k·α_Y, k·α_Z (k=1..5) in 3D
Corresponding projected tick marks in the image plane (unevenly spaced)
Arrows indicating axis directions and annotations
Reference objects or measurement lines (bookshelf height, table base radius) with dimensional labels

interactions.label: Axis to project
interactions.teaches: Selects which world axis to show tick calibration and projection for, illustrating different distortion for each axis in the image

interactions.label: Number of ticks
interactions.teaches: Increases the number of tick marks along the selected axis, showing how perspective affects larger extents

interactions.label: Tick spacing (meters)
interactions.teaches: Adjusts the real-world distance between ticks, relating reference scene measurements to axis calibration

interactions.label: Show reference objects
interactions.teaches: Toggles the display of reference scene measurements used to calibrate each axis scale

demo_steps.title: Intro: Perspective axes and image plane
demo_steps.narration: Here's the 3D world axes (X, Y, Z) and their projection into the image plane. Notice how dashed lines connect equally spaced points in 3D to their projections, which are not evenly spaced in the image—this happens due to perspective distortion.

demo_steps.title: Calibrate an axis using a real measurement
demo_steps.narration: Suppose we know the real height of an object along the Y axis, such as the bookshelf's 1.97 meters. We use its projection to set α_Y—the unit step. With reference enabled, you see the measured segment used to anchor the calibration.

demo_steps.title: See the non-uniform tick spacing in image
demo_steps.narration: Increase the number of ticks. The spacing between ticks stays constant in world coordinates, but their projections get closer together farther from the origin. This geometric effect is a direct result of the cross-ratio invariance.

demo_steps.title: Calibrate another axis with a different reference
demo_steps.narration: Switch to calibrating the Z axis, using the table base radius (0.5 m) as the scale. See how a different axis distorts differently in the image. Reference object lines highlight the anchor measurement on the Z axis.

demo_steps.title: Hide references to focus on grid structure
demo_steps.narration: Now hide the scene references. The relation between world and image tick positions is preserved, but without real-world anchors the system becomes ambiguous up to a global scale. Metric calibration always requires physical knowledge.

interactionPlan.camera_suggestion: Oblique 3D view: camera outside image plane looking slightly down and across axes, so both the 3D axes and their image plane projections are visible, with vanishing points extending. Right side panel is a 2D front-parallel close-up of the image plane with projected axes.
interactionPlan.notes: Dashed projection lines should be highlighted for the currently selected axis. For each axis selection, update tick marks, cross-ratio construction lines, and reference measurements accordingly. Animate changes smoothly (camera interpolation, tick growth, fade on reference objects). Use color consistency: blue=X, red=Y, green=Z. In 2D image view, show tick labels and projected points for selected axis. Use label overlays for α_X, α_Y, α_Z and legend for reference object measurements.

attempts.html:
Axis to project
Demo steps
⚠ Figure failed to load

discrepancies: The right image (b) should appear as a flat 2D projection but is rendered with a slight 3D tilt/perspective, not matching the flat-on style of the source.
Some projected tick spacing on the right panel appears visually more uniform than the strong uneven perspective spacing in the source figure.
In image (a), the labeling for the projection rays and tick marks lacks the precise layout/labels seen in the textbook (e.g., not all projected intersection points are clearly annotated).
The dashed projection rays are subtler and lighter than in the textbook, and the colored vanishing points on the image plane are larger.
A few label placements on (b) overlap with axes or are not perfectly aligned with the target points.
evaluation.notes: The interactive figure is highly faithful, with strong geometry and pedagogical interactivity, but minor alignment and style issues prevent a perfect match.
action_items: Adjust the right image (b) to appear fully 2D/orthographic, matching the flat perspective of the source.
Refine projected tick mark positions in (b) to exaggerate the non-uniform perspective spacing, matching the visual impression of the source.
Tune label positions (especially in image (b)) to avoid overlap and enhance clarity, and ensure all projected points have corresponding labels as in the source.
Slightly darken and thicken dashed projection lines to better match the textbook visual weight.
Reduce the size of colored vanishing point spheres to better match the subtler dot annotations in the original.

feedback.rationale: All action items relate to visual/style adjustments, projection render accuracy, and label placements, indicating the plan is sound but the generation needs refinement.
action_items: Adjust the right image (b) to appear fully 2D/orthographic, matching the flat perspective of the source.
Refine projected tick mark positions in (b) to exaggerate the non-uniform perspective spacing, matching the visual impression of the source.
Tune label positions (especially in image (b)) to avoid overlap and enhance clarity, and ensure all projected points have corresponding labels as in the source.
Slightly darken and thicken dashed projection lines to better match the textbook visual weight.
Reduce the size of colored vanishing point spheres to better match the subtler dot annotations in the original.

Reduce the size of colored vanishing point spheres to better match the subtler dot annotations in the original.

attempts.html:
Axis to project
Demo steps
⚠ Figure failed to load

discrepancies: Reference dimensions (bookshelf height, table base radius) are not present in the original but are added in the generated figure.
Some tick marks and alpha labels are dynamically generated and have a pedagogical overlay not in the original figure.
UI controls and step-through options are present but not part of the source figure.
Horizon line and additional reference vanishing point shown with extra UI labels.
Back face lines, frame doubles and reference helper objects make the generated scene busier than the source.
evaluation.notes: Excellent interactive and pedagogical extension but introduces elements and annotative clutter absent from the original, mildly reducing faithfulness.
action_items: Reduce or make optional the hallucinated reference dimensions (bookshelf height, table base radius, radius reference) to better match the original.
Hide or minimize extra UI and demo steps in default view for higher visual faithfulness.
Harmonize alpha label counts, marker sizes, and placements to match the original axes and points exactly.
Ensure removal of secondary frames and back lines by default to reduce composition clutter.
Retain the advanced interactivity and step-through as an opt-in pedagogical layer, not default.

feedback.rationale: The feedback indicates the overall figure concept and planning are sound but the generated output adds non-original elements and UI clutter that should be minimized or made optional to align visually with the source.
action_items: Reduce or make optional the hallucinated reference dimensions (bookshelf height, table base radius, radius reference) to better match the original.
Hide or minimize extra UI and demo steps in default view for higher visual faithfulness.
Harmonize alpha label counts, marker sizes, and placements to match the original axes and points exactly.
Ensure removal of secondary frames and back lines by default to reduce composition clutter.
Retain the advanced interactivity and step-through as an opt-in pedagogical layer, not default.
actionItems: Reduce or make optional the hallucinated reference dimensions (bookshelf height, table base radius, radius reference) to better match the original.
Hide or minimize extra UI and demo steps in default view for higher visual faithfulness.
Harmonize alpha label counts, marker sizes, and placements to match the original axes and points exactly.
Ensure removal of secondary frames and back lines by default to reduce composition clutter.
Retain the advanced interactivity and step-through as an opt-in pedagogical layer, not default.

attempts.html:
Axis to project
Demo steps
⚠ Figure failed to load

discrepancies: The interactive controls UI partially occludes the left portion of the 3D figure, potentially obscuring visual elements.
Dashed lines and projection lines on the left side appear more saturated and complex than in the source, introducing visual clutter.
Reference demo elements like 'bookshelf height' and 'table base radius' are not present in the source figure.
Tick marks and double-headed arrows (for units) are interactively generated, but visually and proportionally differ from the consistent static marks in the source image.
Fonts and positioning of a few labels (notably on axes and vectors) do not fully match the aesthetic, placement, or clarity of the original.
evaluation.notes: Strong interactive features and core geometry match well, but there are extra reference objects not in the original and some label/style discrepancies.

Adjust tick marks and double-arrow elements to more closely match the static, uniform ticks/arrows in the original diagram.
Move the UI overlay to avoid covering figure content, ensuring all visual elements remain visible.
